matches.match_rationale: The core pain is maintaining dual-format documents (e.g., DepEd curriculum map vs. accreditor curriculum map) that must stay consistent. Text generation can produce format-specific variants of the same underlying content from a single source of truth, reducing manual duplication. For a 500+ staff operator, this directly addresses the 'two versions, must stay consistent' burden.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that accepts a master curriculum map and auto-generates the DepEd-formatted and accreditor-formatted versions on demand, with change propagation when the master is updated.

matches.match_rationale: Compliance documents exist in multiple schemas across DepEd, CHED, PRC, and accreditors — all describing the same underlying institutional data but in different formats and field mappings. Data transformation is precisely the capability for reshaping a single structured dataset into multiple target schemas, eliminating the need to manually maintain parallel document sets. This directly removes the duplication and consistency risk described in the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A schema-mapping layer that holds one canonical compliance record per requirement (curriculum, faculty credentials, facilities) and renders it into any regulator's required format or template on demand.

matches.match_rationale: The operator lacks a unified compliance framework — they cannot see which DepEd requirement overlaps with, conflicts with, or duplicates an accreditor requirement. Knowledge extraction can parse regulatory documents and build a relationship graph: requirement A from DepEd maps to requirement B from the accreditor, with conflict or equivalence relationships annotated. This gives leadership visibility into the full overlap landscape rather than discovering conflicts reactively during inspection.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated regulatory mapping tool that ingests DepEd, CHED, PRC, and accreditor handbooks and outputs a structured cross-reference graph showing overlapping, conflicting, and unique requirements across bodies.

matches.match_rationale: Staff navigating multi-regulatory compliance frequently need to answer questions like 'What does DepEd require for this, and does our accreditor say anything different?' RAG grounds answers in the actual regulatory texts, enabling accurate, citation-backed responses rather than relying on staff memory or manual document searches. This directly addresses the 'no clear guidance' aspect of the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance Q&A assistant trained on all applicable regulatory frameworks that answers questions with citations, flags conflicting requirements from different bodies, and suggests which format or standard takes precedence in context.

matches.match_rationale: The core multi-regulatory problem is finding a single configuration (e.g., a curriculum map format or corrective action plan) that simultaneously satisfies the hard constraints of multiple regulatory bodies — a classic constraint satisfaction problem. When DepEd and an accreditor impose conflicting format requirements, constraint satisfaction can identify whether a unified format exists, flag irreconcilable conflicts, and propose the best compromise configuration.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance configuration engine that takes the constraint sets from each regulator and outputs either a unified-format artifact that satisfies all requirements or a flagged conflict report showing exactly where requirements cannot be reconciled.

matches.match_rationale: Maintaining consistency across multiple regulatory document versions is a multi-step workflow: detect a change in the master document, identify which regulator-specific versions are affected, regenerate those versions, flag for human review, and update the compliance tracker. A task automation agent can own this workflow end-to-end, replacing the manual coordination burden. The 500+ staff scale makes the ROI on automation high.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated compliance document pipeline: when a curriculum map or policy document is updated, the agent identifies all affected regulator-specific formats, regenerates them, runs a consistency check, and queues a human review notification — eliminating manual version tracking.
